summary: CLI pre-recommendation Option (B) vs Claude SELECTION B-MODIFIED
detail: CLI proposed generic Sakai-D6(1)-geometric construction with differentiation against 5 existing constructions. Claude accepted direction but narrowed to Sakai-D6(1) surface-preservation AT ORDER d>=3 with explicit tau-function bilinear identity respecting the Sakai 2-form. Modification is strictly sharper: (i) absorbs Q-v23-1 SHARPER-BOOKKEEPING corollary substrate; (ii) sharper differentiation criterion against 5 existing constructions which are mostly at d=2 or recursion-without-surface-bookkeeping; (iii) creates natural fail-condition operationally checkable. CLI absorbs without counter-amendment.
resolution: Accepted as substrate-improvement; B-MODIFIED is the operative scope going forward

summary: Claude added Amendment-7 not in CLI prompt
detail: CLI prompt requested Amendments 1', 4', 5, 6 only. Claude added Amendment-7 (bibliographic-hallucination-rate-trigger) as forward-discipline addition codifying that any LOAD-BEARING reference list must pass post-031 pre-verification at 0/N FAIL before the carrying claim is banded. This extends existing 'Bibliographic identifier pre-verification' memory from lit-hunt prompts to T1-Synth verdict reference lists. CLI absorbs as memory-promotion candidate UF-189-1.
resolution: Accepted; promoted to memory candidate (see unexpected_finds.json UF-189-1)

summary: Claude flagged Wang-Xu 2025 arXiv:2512.19381 for re-verification at FIRE-A2' launch
detail: Claude noted Wang-Xu 2025 'postdates typical pre-verification windows' and should be re-verified at FIRE-A2' launch. CLI ran abstract pre-fetch at this fire (raw HTML fetch confirms title 'Asymptotic and monodromy problems for higher-order Painlevé III equations'; abstract describes isomonodromic deformation of n*n linear systems with two second-order poles and tt* application; abstract does NOT invoke Sakai-D6(1) surface language). This supports Claude's 'GENUINELY UNCERTAIN pending read' assessment; full pre-read at FIRE-A2' remains required.
resolution: Wang-Xu 2025 partially pre-verified at slot 189; full read deferred to FIRE-A2'

summary: Verdict BAND = MEDIUM (no dual-witness escalation required for absorption)
detail: CLI prompt's UF-188-2-derived dual-witness-escalation guard: 'if Claude flags any new LOAD-BEARING claim under LOW or LOW-MEDIUM band, recommended ceiling = MEDIUM; load-bearing claims at LOW band require dual-witness escalation before downstream substrate work proceeds.' Claude returned BAND=MEDIUM with explicit acknowledgment: 'This consultation verdict itself is single-witness MEDIUM-band, but the load-bearing claim it carries (Amendment-1') is correctly flagged for downstream dual-witness rather than being banded at the verdict's overall band.' This is the textbook correct application of load-bearing-discipline per UF-188-2.
resolution: Single-witness MEDIUM-band absorption acceptable; downstream dual-witness mandate on FIRE-A3' / FIRE-A4' inherits per Amendment-4'

detail: Original Q-185-4 was scoped against Amendment-1's LOW-MEDIUM banded broad-gap claim. Under Amendment-1's falsification and Amendment-1' load-bearing-pending-verify status, timing budget must absorb FIRE-A2' + FIRE-A3' chain before any v2.3 milestone-cadence pressure applies.
